seo.title: Multi-Agent Coordination Failure in DSPy Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix multi-agent coordination failure in DSPy agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In DSPy, Multi-Agent Coordination Failure occurs when multiple DSPy modules or agents fail to synchronize outputs, leading to cascading errors in complex pipelines. This manifests as mismatched schemas between agent outputs and downstream module expectations, or agents producing uncoordinated responses that violate shared state assumptions. DSPy's optimizable nature exacerbates this, as individual module optimization can create incompatible output distributions across agents.

howItManifests.scenario: Sequential agent pipeline where Agent1 generates topics and Agent2 expands them
howItManifests.symptom: Agent2 receives partial or malformed topic lists due to Agent1's variable output formatting
howItManifests.impact: Downstream summarization module crashes with parsing errors or produces incomplete summaries

howItManifests.scenario: Parallel researcher agents merging findings into a shared report
howItManifests.symptom: Agents produce overlapping or contradictory facts without deduplication logic
howItManifests.impact: Final report contains redundant or conflicting information, reducing factual accuracy

howItManifests.scenario: Debate-style agents where critic and generator fail to reference prior turns
howItManifests.symptom: Each agent ignores previous responses, creating disjoint reasoning chains
howItManifests.impact: No convergence on optimal solution despite multiple iterations

howItManifests.scenario: Multi-agent RAG system with retriever, synthesizer, and validator agents
howItManifests.symptom: Validator agent receives unsynchronized context chunks from parallel retrievers
howItManifests.impact: Fact-checking fails due to incomplete or out-of-order evidence presentation

fixes.name: Shared State Signature
fixes.description: DSPy Signature that enforces consistent state passing between agents

fixes.name: Output Normalizer Module
fixes.description: Standardizes agent outputs to prevent schema drift

fixes.name: Coordinated MultiAgent Optimizer
fixes.description: Compiles entire multi-agent system with joint optimization
fixes.code:
import dspy

class CoordinatedMultiAgent(dspy.Module):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.agent1 = dspy.ChainOfThought('task -> state1')
        self.agent2 = dspy.ChainOfThought('task, state1 -> state2')
        self.validator = dspy.ChainOfThought('task, state1, state2 -> final')
    
    def forward(self, example):
        state1 = self.agent1(task=example.task)
        state2 = self.agent2(task=example.task, state1=state1.output)
        final = self.validator(
            task=example.task,
            state1=state1.output,
            state2=state2.output
        )
        return dspy.Prediction(output=final.output)

# Joint optimization
optimizer = dspy.MIPROv2(
    CoordinatedMultiAgent(), 
    metric=lambda ex, pred, trace: trace.final.score
)

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Multi-Agent Coordination Failure Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex automates detection by wrapping DSPy module forward() calls with vex.verify(), intercepting runtime failures like schema mismatches and state inconsistencies. It captures agent traces across the entire pipeline, identifying coordination failures that manifest as assertion violations or unexpected output distributions. Vex's DSPy integration provides automatic remediation suggestions, including signature adjustments and module rewiring.

For multi-agent systems, Vex monitors inter-module handoffs, validating shared state consistency and output schemas in real-time. When failures occur, Vex generates DSPy-compatible fixes like normalized output modules or coordinated signatures, then re-optimizes the pipeline. This eliminates manual debugging of complex agent interactions.

Integration requires pip install dspy vex-sdk, then wrapping modules with @vex.verify decorator. Vex handles both development-time validation and production monitoring.
